controllers.name: 8BitDo 64 (Funtastic)
controllers.tagline: N64-inspired Bluetooth controller for the Analogue 3D and Switch, with Hall Effect sticks and a metal joystick ring.

controllers.name: Ultimate 2C Wireless (PC)
controllers.tagline: Budget Xbox-layout controller for PC and Android. Hall Effect sticks at $29.99.
controllers._curatorNotes: Per the 2C-wireless-vs-C-wireless comparison table: Pro Back Paddles, 3.5mm Audio Jack, 6-axis Motion, Vibration, Turbo, RGB Fire Ring, Charging Dock, Ultimate Software all rendered as empty in markdown. Treated as 'not present' (booleanByDefault). The page's feature copy confirms Hall Effect sticks+triggers, refined bumpers, custom mapping, mode switch — no mention of vibration/motion/RGB/dock.

controllers.name: Pro 2 (Special Edition)
controllers.tagline: Special-edition colourways of the Hall Effect Pro 2, with broad platform support and on-the-fly profiles.

controllers.name: 8BitDo 64 (Prototype)
controllers.tagline: Prototype-themed limited editions of the 64 Bluetooth controller; same hardware, transparent colourways.

controllers.name: Ultimate 2 Wireless
controllers.tagline: Xbox-layout flagship for PC/Mac/SteamOS/Android. NOTE: does NOT support Nintendo Switch.

Triggers.value: Dual-mode: Linear Hall Effect / Non-linear tactile

controllers._curatorNotes: Major naming hazard: 'Ultimate 2 Wireless' has NO Switch support, while 'Ultimate 2 Bluetooth' (a separate product) DOES. The comparison view should surface this asymmetry clearly in the Compatibility row. Battery capacity not listed on the page comparison table for this product.

controllers.name: Pro 3
controllers.tagline: Retro flagship with TMR sticks, Hall Effect triggers, swappable ABXY, and an integrated charging dock.

Layout.value: Switch layout (swappable magnetic ABXY: Switch or Xbox)

Polling Rate.value: D-input: BT 60-100Hz, Wired 100-200Hz; S-input: BT 60-200Hz, Wired/2.4G 125Hz; X-input: Wired/2.4G 250Hz

Triggers.value: Dual-mode: Linear Hall Effect / Non-linear micro-switch

controllers._curatorNotes: 3.5mm Audio Jack and RGB Fire Ring rows are present in the on-page comparison table but rendered as empty cells; not mentioned in feature copy either way. Conservatively omitted (= 'no' in the comparison view per booleanByDefault).

controllers.name: Pro 2 (Hall Effect)
controllers.tagline: Previous-generation Pro flagship, now with Hall Effect sticks. Broadest platform support including Raspberry Pi.

Polling Rate.value: D-input: BT 60Hz, Wired 100Hz; S-input: BT 60-200Hz, Wired 100Hz; X-input: Wired 100Hz

controllers._curatorNotes: Per Pro 3 comparison table: Pro 2 does NOT have Fast Bumpers (L4/R4), Charging Dock, or RGB Fire Ring (those rows are Pro-3-only). Switch 2 support added via firmware update. Switch compatibility does not support HD rumble or amiibo scanning.

controllers.name: Ultimate 2 Wireless (Honkai: Star Rail – Evernight)
controllers.tagline: Officially-licensed Honkai: Star Rail edition of the Ultimate 2 Wireless. PC/Mac/SteamOS/Android — no Switch support.
controllers.storeTitle: 8BitDo Ultimate 2 Wireless Controller (Honkai: Star Rail - Evernight)

controllers.name: Ultimate 2 Bluetooth
controllers.tagline: Switch-layout flagship for Switch + PC. Counterpart to the (PC-only) Ultimate 2 Wireless.

Triggers.value: Dual-mode: Linear Hall Effect / Non-linear tactile

controllers._curatorNotes: Major naming hazard (see Ultimate 2 Wireless entry). Polling Rate row was present on the comparison table but rendered as empty — likely 2.4G/Wired 1000Hz to match the rest of the line. Confirm before shipping.

controllers.name: SN30 Pro (Hall Effect)
controllers.tagline: SNES-style retro flagship, now with Hall Effect sticks. Broad platform support including Raspberry Pi.
controllers._curatorNotes: Older product, no embedded comparison table on its marketing page — specs above come from the page's hero/feature copy ('Hall Effect joysticks', 'rumble vibration', 'motion controls', 'wireless bluetooth', 'rechargeable battery', 'USB-C connector'). Battery capacity, polling rate, Ultimate Software support, and back paddles all unclear from the page; left out rather than guessed. Switch 2 support added via firmware update. Switch compatibility doesn't support HD rumble or amiibo.

controllers.name: Ultimate 2C (Black Myth: Wukong)
controllers.tagline: Black Myth: Wukong edition of the Ultimate 2C. Budget Hall Effect controller for PC and Android.

controllers.name: 8BitDo SN30 2.4G
controllers.tagline: 2.4G wireless SNES/SFC-style pad for the original Super NES, Super Famicom and Analogue Super Nt (USB on Switch/PC).

Connectivity.value: 2.4G (SNES / SFC / Super Nt); Wired USB (Switch / Windows)

controllers.name: Ultimate 2C Wired
controllers.tagline: Cheapest entry, wired-only. Hall Effect sticks at $19.99.
controllers._curatorNotes: Same pattern as 2C Wireless — all premium features (back paddles, motion, vibration, turbo, RGB, audio jack) are absent from the comparison table and feature copy; treated as not present. No battery section since it's wired-only.

controllers.name: Lite 2
controllers.tagline: Ultra-portable, fully-featured small-form-factor controller. Made for 2D games and travel.

6-axis Motion Control.value: Yes, but only in S (Switch) mode

Vibration.value: Yes, but only in S (Switch) mode (no HD rumble on Switch)

controllers._curatorNotes: Does NOT officially support Windows/PC directly per the 8bitdo.com page (Switch, Android, Apple, Raspberry Pi only). Some users report D-input working with PC via Bluetooth but it's unsupported. Ultimate Software is not in its supported-devices list. For Switch+PC+Mac users, this controller MISSES PC support.
